meta.periodization: A=Hypertrophy, B=Volume, C=Strength, D=Power, E=Powerbuilding, F=Metabolic
sources: Built With Science / Dr. Eric Trexler

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.note: Primary horizontal compound — do first when freshest

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Upper chest + anterior delt — incline hits fibres flat misses

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.note: Primary vertical compound — front delt + upper chest overlap

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
exercises.note: Lateral delt isolation — the only way to widen shoulders

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Ext
exercises.note: Long head stretched position — 40% more growth than pushdowns (2023)

exercises.name: Tricep Pushdowns
exercises.note: Lateral head isolation — cable keeps tension at peak

exercises.name: Incline Barbell Press
exercises.note: Upper chest emphasis — fresh angle from flat bench

exercises.name: Cable Fly
exercises.note: Constant tension through full ROM — peak contraction flat bench misses

exercises.name: Arnold Press
exercises.note: Rotation recruits all 3 delt heads — more complete than standard press

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Cable keeps tension at peak vs dumbbell which drops off

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Ext
exercises.note: Long head anchor — higher reps this block for volume

exercises.name: Dips
exercises.note: Bodyweight compound — tricep + lower chest, high metabolic cost

variants.label: C
variants.repScheme: 4-6 reps, 80-85% 1RM — resensitizes muscles for next hypertrophy block

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.note: Heaviest vertical press of the cycle

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench
exercises.note: Compound tricep + chest — heaviest tricep work

exercises.name: Decline Barbell Press
exercises.note: Lower pec + more load than incline — completes chest coverage

exercises.name: Skull Crushers
exercises.note: Long head strength in stretched position

exercises.name: Rear Delt Fly
exercises.note: Isolation only — keeps shoulder health during heavy pressing block

variants.label: D
variants.repScheme: Mixed 6-10 reps, explosive and novel movements

exercises.name: KB Clean and Press
exercises.note: Athletic compound — hip drive to overhead, novel motor pattern

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
exercises.note: Eliminates shoulder stretch at bottom — novel stimulus vs bench

exercises.name: Pec Deck / Machine Fly
exercises.note: Constant tension machine isolation — safe to go heavy

exercises.name: Shrugs
exercises.note: Upper trap — posture + shoulder health support

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Ext
exercises.note: Long head anchor — heavier than B block

exercises.name: Tricep Kickbacks
exercises.note: Peak contraction at full extension — novel angle

variants.label: E
variants.repScheme: 5-8 reps on compounds, 8-12 on accessories — strength + size together

exercises.name: Bench Press
exercises.note: Heavier than hypertrophy block — builds both size and strength

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Accessory volume after heavy main lift

exercises.name: Cable Lateral Raise
exercises.note: Isolation volume — maintains delt size during strength focus

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench
exercises.note: Compound tricep strength — pairs well with bench day

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Ext
exercises.note: Long head volume after compound strength work

variants.label: F
variants.repScheme: 12-20 reps, shorter rest, circuit-style — max calorie burn + conditioning

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.note: High rep bodyweight — keeps heart rate elevated

exercises.name: Dumbbell Floor Press
exercises.note: Light dumbbell, fast tempo — metabolic chest work

exercises.name: Lateral Raises
exercises.note: Light weight high rep — shoulder endurance + burn

exercises.name: Pike Push-Ups
exercises.note: Bodyweight vertical push — no rest needed between

exercises.name: Diamond Push-Ups
exercises.note: Bodyweight tricep — pairs as superset with pike push-ups

exercises.name: Dips
exercises.note: High rep dips for metabolic tricep + chest stimulus

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: King of posterior chain — erectors, glutes, traps, lats

exercises.name: Barbell Row
exercises.note: Horizontal thickness compound — mid back mass

exercises.name: Pull-Ups
exercises.note: Vertical pull — lat width, long head bicep

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.note: Rear delt + rotator cuff — essential shoulder health

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Long head stretch at bottom — greater ROM than standing

variants.label: B
variants.repScheme: 10-15 reps, lighter load, higher total sets

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.note: Vertical pull — easier to feel lat vs pull-up for volume

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.note: Horizontal thickness — cable keeps tension throughout

exercises.name: Straight-Arm Pulldown
exercises.note: Pure lat isolation — elbows locked, no bicep involvement

exercises.name: Single-Arm DB Row
exercises.note: Unilateral — fixes imbalances, greater ROM than barbell

exercises.name: Cable Curl
exercises.note: Constant tension through full ROM — better than dumbbell for volume

exercises.name: Concentration Curl
exercises.note: Highest bicep EMG of any curl — peak contraction isolation

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: Primary strength pull - max posterior chain load

exercises.name: T-Bar Row
exercises.note: Thick mid-back compound - neutral grip reduces bicep fatigue

exercises.name: Pendlay Row
exercises.note: Dead-stop barbell row - eliminates momentum, pure back strength

exercises.name: Chest Supported Row
exercises.note: Isolates mid-back without lower back fatigue - go heavy

exercises.name: Reverse Curl
exercises.note: Brachialis + forearm strength - supports all heavy pulling

exercises.name: Preacher Curl
exercises.note: Eliminates cheating - pure bicep strength at bottom range

variants.label: D
variants.repScheme: Mixed rep ranges, explosive and novel movements

exercises.name: KB Swing
exercises.note: Hip hinge power - posterior chain conditioning + calorie burn

exercises.name: Single-Arm DB Row
exercises.note: Unilateral - max ROM, fixes imbalances, heavy load possible

exercises.name: Meadows Row
exercises.note: Landmine row - unique angle hits lower lat and rear delt hard

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row (Wide Grip)
exercises.note: Wide grip shifts load to upper back and rear delts

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Curl
exercises.note: Long head stretch - greater ROM than standing curl

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: Strength focus — heavier than A block, longer rest

exercises.name: Barbell Row
exercises.note: Heavy horizontal compound — pairs with deadlift strength day

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown
exercises.note: Accessory vertical pull after heavy compounds

exercises.name: Face Pulls
exercises.note: Shoulder health — never skip regardless of goal

exercises.name: Hammer Curls
exercises.note: Heavier than hypertrophy block — brachialis strength

exercises.name: KB Swing
exercises.note: High rep swing - posterior chain conditioning + calorie burn

exercises.name: Seated Cable Row
exercises.note: High rep horizontal pull - constant cable tension

exercises.name: Lat Pulldown (Wide Grip)
exercises.note: High rep vertical pull - lat width endurance

exercises.name: Single-Arm DB Row
exercises.note: High rep unilateral - metabolic back work

exercises.name: Band Bicep Curl
exercises.note: Light band high rep - bicep pump and endurance

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.note: King of leg exercises — quad, glute, erector compound

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Quad compound — safely overload quads after squats

exercises.name: Leg Curl
exercises.note: Hamstring isolation — fills gap hip hinge misses at knee

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Highest glute EMG exercise — glute dominant at lockout

exercises.name: Sumo Deadlift
exercises.note: Wide stance shifts load to adductors and glutes vs conventional

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.note: Unilateral — fixes imbalances, high metabolic cost

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.note: Eccentric overload — highest hamstring damage for growth

exercises.name: Seated Calf Raise
exercises.note: Soleus emphasis — different muscle than standing calf raise

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.note: Neutral grip, safer heavy pull — more quad than conventional

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: Unilateral strength — very high quad and glute load

exercises.name: Hack Squat
exercises.note: Machine compound — quad emphasis, safe when fatigued

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.note: Heavier than A/B — strength reps for gastrocnemius

exercises.name: Box Jumps
exercises.note: Plyometric — trains rate of force development

exercises.name: KB Goblet Squat
exercises.note: Front-loaded squat — more upright torso, quad dominant

exercises.name: Single-Leg RDL
exercises.note: Unilateral hip hinge — balance + hamstring stretch

exercises.name: Donkey Calf Raise
exercises.note: Hip flexed stretches gastrocnemius further than standing

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.note: Heavier than hypertrophy block — strength + size together

exercises.name: Romanian Deadlift
exercises.note: Heavy hip hinge — hamstring strength and mass

exercises.name: Leg Press
exercises.note: Heavy accessory — quad volume after strength squats

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Glute strength compound — heavier than volume block

exercises.name: Calf Raises
exercises.note: Strength-rep calves — heavier load than hypertrophy blocks

exercises.name: Jump Squat
exercises.note: Plyometric — high calorie burn, full leg activation

exercises.name: Walking Lunges
exercises.note: Continuous movement — keeps heart rate elevated

exercises.name: Glute Bridge
exercises.note: High rep posterior chain — minimal rest between sets

exercises.name: Skater Jumps
exercises.note: Lateral plyometric — hip abductor power and conditioning

exercises.name: Box Jumps
exercises.note: Explosive finisher — max calorie burn per rep

exercises.name: Donkey Calf Raise
exercises.note: High rep burnout — calf endurance finisher

exercises.name: Squat
exercises.note: Lower body compound first — most demanding movement

exercises.name: Overhead Press
exercises.note: Vertical push — shoulder + upper chest + tricep

exercises.name: Hammer Curls
exercises.note: Brachialis — arm thickness without fatiguing back for rows

exercises.name: Overhead Tricep Ext
exercises.note: Long head — 40% more growth than pressdowns

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: Full posterior chain — requires most neural resources, do first

exercises.name: Incline Dumbbell Press
exercises.note: Upper chest + shoulder — complements deadlift day well

exercises.name: Pull-Ups
exercises.note: Vertical pull — lat width after deadlift

exercises.name: Bulgarian Split Squat
exercises.note: Unilateral — high quad/glute stimulus without fatiguing spine

exercises.name: Skull Crushers
exercises.note: Long head stretch — pairs with curl as superset option

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.note: Safer heavy pull for full body day — less low back fatigue

exercises.name: Close-Grip Bench
exercises.note: Compound tricep strength — also hits chest

exercises.name: Reverse Curl
exercises.note: Brachialis strength — supports all compound pulling

variants.label: D
variants.repScheme: Mixed rep ranges, novel motor patterns

exercises.name: Trap Bar Deadlift
exercises.note: Athletic hinge — neutral grip, balanced load

exercises.name: KB Clean and Press
exercises.note: Full body power — hip drive to overhead

exercises.name: Hip Thrust
exercises.note: Glute compound — novel stimulus vs squat pattern

exercises.name: Cable Fly
exercises.note: Constant tension — novel vs pressing movements

exercises.name: Deadlift
exercises.note: Strength pull — fewer reps, more weight than A/B

exercises.name: Hammer Curls
exercises.note: Heavier brachialis work than hypertrophy block

exercises.name: Jump Squat
exercises.note: Plyometric lower body — high calorie burn

exercises.name: Push-Ups
exercises.note: Bodyweight push — keeps heart rate up

exercises.name: KB Swing
exercises.note: Hip hinge power + conditioning — full posterior chain

exercises.name: Burpee
exercises.note: Full body conditioning — highest calorie burn per rep

exercises.name: Mountain Climbers
exercises.note: Dynamic core + hip flexor — metabolic finisher
